public String lastSubstring(String s) {
    int maxChar = 'a';
    for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
        if (s.charAt(i) > maxChar) {
            maxChar = s.charAt(i);
        }
    }
    int firstIndex = -1;
    for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
        if (s.charAt(i) == maxChar) {
            firstIndex = i;
            break;
        }
    }
    return s.substring(firstIndex);
}

This should be efficient, O(n) time and O(1) space.

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public String lastSubstring(String s) {
        int maxChar = 'a';
        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
            if (s.charAt(i) > maxChar) {
                maxChar = s.charAt(i);
            }
        }
        int firstIndex = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
            if (s.charAt(i) == maxChar) {
                firstIndex = i;
                break;
            }
        }
        return s.substring(firstIndex);
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution efficiently finds the lexicographically largest substring by identifying the highest character and returning the longest substring starting from its first occurrence. This approach ensures optimal performance with linear time complexity.
